repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
According to the #7563, I am proposing this PR, which changes the Sphinx documentation theme to Clarity.

Cython docs with this PR applied are deployed for reviewing purposes at https://showcase.readcraft.io/cython/

The announcement top banner with the "go to stable version" link will work when actually deployed; now it intentionally returns 404.

This closes #7563.

comment_data.body: We've had docs build failures in the past due to varying dependency versions, so I'd prefer having a complete freeze here again.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,112 +1,7 @@
-sphinx==7.2.6
+sphinx==9.1.0
 sphinx-issues==5.0.1
-sphinx-tabs==3.4.4
-Jinja2==3.1.3
-jupyter==1.0.0
-# automatic requirements from pip freeze below:

comment_data.body:
Oh, understand. Pinning only direct dependencies is a partial solution. Transitive dependencies can still change. -> I've listed the indirect dependencies too.

(I suggest in some upcoming cleanup task, to use either pip-tools (`pip-compile doc-requirements.in` -> doc-requirements.txt), or the uv with native lock file support.)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,112 +1,7 @@
-sphinx==7.2.6
+sphinx==9.1.0
 sphinx-issues==5.0.1
-sphinx-tabs==3.4.4
-Jinja2==3.1.3
-jupyter==1.0.0
-# automatic requirements from pip freeze below:

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
      PYTHON_VERSION: "3.9"
```

Unless there's a good reason that I've missed I'd propose not making this change here.

(If there is a good reason to do the upgrade now, then add "fstring.pyx" to the list of exclusions in runtest.py on line 2007)

> (If there is a good reason to do the upgrade now, then add "fstring.pyx" to the list of exclusions in runtest.py on line 2007)

Just a curiosity. Why is it needed?

> Why is it needed?

Codestyle uses Python's own parser/tokenizer to interpret the files. The example that's failing in fstring.pyx uses syntax that isn't valid in Python 3.9. So in Python 3.9 just fails to read it and ignores the line. The specific line is bad style, but it's something we want to test (and `noqa` doesn't work).

> The example that's failing in fstring.pyx uses syntax that isn't valid in Python 3.9.

Actually I don't think that's true on a second look.

I think the basic idea that it uses Python's parser/tokenizer is right though. But I don't know exactly why it's different with a different version of Python.
I'm a bit lost now :-) Is there anything I can do in this PR?
Either change the code-style run back to 3.9 (if that works - @matusvalo thinks that it doesn't) or add "fstring.pyx" to the list of exclusions in runtest.py on line 2007.

I unfortunately can't keep Python 3.9. The minimum Python version that should work is 3.11, but it will also reach its end of life in a year. So if you agree, let me keep 3.13 and add `fstring.pyx` to exclusions.

commit.message: ci: update to Python 3.13 for RTD

fix sphinx tabs styling

by updating docs theme Clarity to 2.1.0 which includes styles for Sphinx Tabs extension already
ci: update dependencies for codestyle job
ci: freeze doc dependencies for reproducibility
ci: update to Python 3.13 for building docs
style: ignore fstring.pyx to pass runcodestyle.py with Python 3.13
